content: Loans Personal Loan Personal Loan Eligibility Calculator Eligibility FAQs Personal loan Eligibility FAQs Eligibility & Documentation EXPAND ALL What factors determine personal loan eligibility Eligibility depends on several key factors: • Credit Score (CIBIL) - Higher score leads to stronger eligibility. • Income level -Higher income makes you eligible for higher loan amount. • Employment type - Salaried makes you more eligible than Self-employed for instant loans. • Employer category - Category A companies get best offers. • Debt-to-Income ratio (DTI) - DTI < 45–50% preferred. • Age of 20–58 years is typical range. • Banking behavior - Stable deposits and no cheque bounces. What is the eligibility for salaried vs self-employed borrowers? Salaried Eligibility: • Salary: ₹20,000+ • Bureau score: 700+ • Salary credited to bank account • Minimal documentation Self-employed Eligibility: • Business vintage: 2-3 years • Bank statement with consistent cash flow • CIBIL score: 700+ • Latest ITR/GST returns What is the minimum salary needed for personal loan? Salary requirement differs across lenders: Banks: Minimum ₹20,000–₹25,000/month How age affects personal loan eligibility? Age is a key factor that affects personal loan elligibility. Minimum age: • 20 years Maximum age at loan maturity: • 55–58 years Younger applicants: Longer tenure increases higher eligibility Older applicants: Shorter tenure lowers loan amounts Does job type affect eligibility? Yes, job type influences risk assessment. Best job types (highest eligibility): • Government employees • MNC employees Moderate: • Private sector with stable company • Self-employed professionals (CA, doctor, lawyer) Higher risk: • Contractual workers • Cash-based jobs • Small business owners Job stability reduces risk for lenders. Eligibility criteria for govt employees. Government employees have some of the highest approval rates. Benefits: Lower interest rates Higher loan limits Faster approval Eligibility: Age: 20–58 years Salary: ₹20,000 minimum CIBIL score: 700+ 6 months job stability Aadhaar + PAN Salary account Govt employees fall under Category A or B in most banks. How to increase personal loan eligibility? Proven ways to increase eligibility: • Improve CIBIL score • Reduce existing EMIs • Choose longer tenure • Apply with salary account bank • Increase income (salary hike, bonus etc.) • Add a co-applicant • Maintain good banking behavior • Avoid enquiry spam • Keep credit card utilization below 30% Better profile increases chance of higher loan amount and lower interest. Eligibility for high-value loan (₹10–20 lakh). ICICI Bank offers loans up to ₹50 lakh. To qualify for ₹10–20 lakh, you typically need: - Monthly income above ₹30,000 - Strong credit score (700+) - Low income to debt ratio What is the loan eligibility based on income vs expenses? Eligibility depends on net disposable income, not just income. Lenders check: Net monthly income Existing EMIs Fixed obligations (Debt to income ratio)  What is the eligibility for salaried vs self-employed borrowers?
